data.title: BriaCell Therapeutics announces proposed public offering, shares fall
data.description:
• BriaCell Therapeutics (NASDAQ:BCTX) said on Wednesday it intends to offer to sell common shares and warrants in an underwritten public offering.
  • All the common shares and warrants are to be sold by the company.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from the offering primarily for working capital requirements, general corporate purposes, and the advancement of business objectives.
  • BriaCell Therapeutics (BCTX) shares fell more than 22% in post-market trading.

data.title: Chewy announces public offering of Class A common stock by selling stockholder
data.description:
• Chewy (NYSE:CHWY) on Wednesday announced the commencement of an underwritten public offering of $500 million of shares of its Class A common stock by selling stockholder.
  • The selling stockholder intends to grant the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional $75 million of shares of Class A Common Stock.
  • Chewy will not sell any shares of its Class A Common Stock in the offering and will not receive any proceeds.
  • In addition, Chewy has agreed to purchase from the selling stockholder $50 million of Class A Common Stock at a price per share equal to the per share purchase price to be paid by the underwriters in the offering.
  • The concurrent repurchase is being executed separately from the company’s existing $500 million share repurchase program authorized on May 24, 2024, which will be unaffected by this transaction.
  • The concurrent repurchase is expected to be consummated concurrently with the offering.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Pentagon does not know where mysterious drones are coming from: report
data.description:
• The Pentagon said on Wednesday it does not know where the drones that have been sighted over parts of New Jersey, New York and Pennsylvania are coming from, according to CBS News.
  • “At this time, we have no evidence that these activities are coming from a foreign adversary,” Pentagon press secretary Sabrina Singh said in a press briefing, according to the news outlet. “We’re going to continue to monitor what is happening but, at no point were our installations threatened when this activity was occurring.”
  • The objects, some of which are reported to be car-sized or SUV-sized in length, are not U.S. military drones, Singh added. She also refuted they were coming from an Iranian ship.
  • Related tickers: AeroVironment (AVA), Boeing (BA), Northrop Grumman (NOC), Lockheed Martin (LMT), L3Harris (LHX), Kratos Defense & Security (KTOS)

data.title: CGI merges operations with Daugherty to expand in multiple U.S. metro markets
data.description:
• CGI (NYSE:GIB) on Wednesday, announced the signature of an equity purchase agreement to merge operations with Daugherty, a St. Louis-based professional services firm specializing in artificial intelligence, data analytics, strategic IT consulting, and business advisory services for Fortune 500 clients.
  • The merger of operations brings more than 1,100 consultants to CGI, further strengthening the company's presence in multiple key U.S. growth markets, including St. Louis, Atlanta, Minneapolis, Chicago, Columbus, Dallas and New York.
  • The parties entered into a purchase agreement on November 29, 2024, and the transaction is expected to close in December 2024.
  • CGI (GIB) shares were up 0.33% in post-market trading.

data.title: Bank of America CEO sees net interest income moving up the ladder from $14.3B in Q4
data.description:
Bank of America (NYSE:BAC) Chairman and CEO Brian Moynihan feels good about net interest income reaching $14.3B in Q4, vs. $14.1B in Q3 and $13.9B in Q2, he said at the Goldman Sachs U.S. Financial Services Conference on Wednesday.

"But the important thing is we look at next quarter, you see another move up the steps," he said. "What you're seeing now is just quarterly progression up the ladder. And that number will continue to go up."

That's due to a couple of factors. The company is expecting loan growth of over 4% in Q4, better than the industry, with the commercial side doing a little better than that. In addition, he's expecting deposits to rise about $30B in Q3, marking its sixth straight quarter of growth. Just as important, though, high-cost deposits are running off.

"The rate structure we have, it basically follows the market," Moynihan said. "So our view is that we'll see good increases sequentially every quarter for next year and just keep building on that ladder."

Wealth management fees in Q4 should be up 20% Y/Y, and investment banking fees are expected to increase 25% Y/Y. Trading revenue will likely be up mid-single to high-single digits from last year, logging in a record Q4, he said.

Credit costs are expected to be flattish, he added.

Bank of America (NYSE:BAC) stock rose 0.7% in late Wednesday trading.

data.title: Logility Supply Chain soars on report it's exploring a sale
data.description:
Logility Supply Chain (NASDAQ:LGTY) jumped 8% after a report that the company is exploring a potential sale.

Logility (LGTY) is working with an investment bank to evaluate a potential sale, according to a Reuters report on Wednesday, which cited people familiar with the matter.

A possible sale comes after a shareholder on Monday called on the company to explore strategic alternatives, including a sale. 2717 Partners LP wants the company to evaluate alternatives, including a sale, the holder wrote in a letter to Logility's (LGTY) board on Monday.

Logility (LGTY) has a market cap of $373 million.

"We believe that if the status quo persists and Logility continues to operate in the public markets, the Company will be unable to achieve its long-term goals for all stakeholders," Edward Robson, 2717 Partners' Chief Investment Officer wrote in the letter. "Logility should find a new home in the private markets."

data.title: Walgreens drops as analysts skeptical over potential sale
data.description:
Walgreens Boots (NASDAQ:WBA) fell 7.3% as investors and analysts were skeptical about a potential sale of the drugstore chain to private equity firm Sycamore Partners

Shares of Walgreens (WBA) reversed court on Wednesday after surging 18% on Tuesday after the WSJ reported that Walgreens and Sycamore have been discussing a deal that could be completed early next year.

"While we acknowledge the context around a potential sale in a challenging pharmacy backdrop, a buyout is harder to contemplate given its already sizeable debt burden and paltry cash flow, making the value creation pathway harder to decipher," Morgan Stanley analyst Erin Wright, wrote in a note on Wednesday.

Wright highlighted Walgreen's (WBA) $9.5 billion of outstanding debt, ~$23 billion of operating leasing and ~$7 billion liability associated with opioid-related claims that could prove an impediment to a possible deal.

"We also see few logical strategic suitors in the wings amidst aforementioned pressures across both front end and pharmacy," Wright, who has an underweight rating and $7 price target on WBA wrote.

Raymond James analyst John Ransom also had issues with the math of a potential Walgreens (WBA) deal.

"We were surprised by this headline as we struggle to see how such a deal could get financed given WBA’s deteriorating earnings/cash flow outlook and ~$15B of liabilities on the balance sheet ($8.2B of net debt + $6.6B of opioid liabilities) along with other liabilities (~$2.1B for store closure costs) and a massive working capital deficit (~$8B)," Ransom, who has a market perform on WBA wrote in a note on Wednesday.

data.title: Apple working with Broadcom to develop in-house AI server chips: report
data.description:
Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) is working with Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO) to develop the company's first in-house server chip exclusively designed for artificial intelligence, The Information reported.

The chip, known internally as Baltra, could be ready for mass production by 2026, the news outlet reported, citing people with direct knowledge of the matter. The new AI chip could be used for inferencing, which has the chip capable of processing new data and then hands them off to the large language models to produce an output, a task different than training models, the news outlet added.

The chip will be produced by Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) and likely use its 3 nanometer process, The Information added, citing sources.

Broadcom shares rose 6.5% in late trading, while Apple shares slipped fractionally. Nvidia (NVDA), which dominates the AI accelerator market, rose 3.5%.

Julian Lin, Investing Group Leader for Best Of Breed Growth Stocks, said the potential partnership is likely to benefit Broadcom more than Apple, as "the latter has historically in-sourced a lot of its technology."

"We might be seeing the predictable next stage of the AI hype cycle: high margins always attracts competition," Lin added.

The plan to bring its own chips to its own servers was born three years ago, but the plan was accelerated after OpenAI's ChatGPT chatbot took the world by storm in December 2022, according to previous reports.

Chief Executive Tim Cook has said previously Apple has some advantages that its competitors in the AI space do not. "We believe in the transformative power and promise of AI, and we believe we have advantages that will differentiate us in this new era, including Apple's unique combination of seamless hardware, software and services integration, groundbreaking Apple's silicon, with our industry-leading neural engines and our unwavering focus on privacy, which underpins everything we create," Cook said on the company's earnings call in May.

Last week, Apple executive Benoit Dupin gave a rare endorsement of Amazon's (AMZN) custom artificial intelligence chips and said the iPhone maker is evaluating Amazon's latest AI chip for pre-training models.

Cupertino, Calif.-based Apple has also used some of Google's (GOOG) (GOOGL) chips to train AI models for Apple Intelligence.

The news comes as Apple continued to roll out its Apple Intelligence features on Wednesday. The tech giant released the latest versions of iOS, macOS and iPadOS, all of which include several AI-related features, including the integration of ChatGPT into Siri.

Apple and Broadcom did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

(This story has been updated to include analyst commentary in the fourth and fifth paragraphs.)
